paths.name: crates_io_dependency
paths.title: crates.io dependency adoption
paths.description: Use RustUse crates as ordinary Cargo dependencies from crates.io, with facade crates for broad domains and child crates for focused primitives.
maintenance_notes: Prefer this path when users want normal semver updates and cargo-managed dependency resolution.
Facade crates should make useful feature-gated entry points without forcing full dependency surfaces.

paths.name: copy_and_own
paths.title: copy-and-own adoption
paths.description: Copy small RustUse primitives into an application or library, then own the local source like shadcn/ui-style code adoption.
maintenance_notes: This works best for tiny primitives with clear tests and docs.
Optional rustuse.toml can record copied primitives and source versions.
Optional rustuse-lock.toml can track the exact copied source for future audits.

paths.name: cli_assisted
paths.title: CLI-assisted adoption via rustuse / cargo-rustuse
paths.description: Use a RustUse CLI flow to discover, select, copy, and maintain primitives from RustUse sets.
maintenance_notes: The v0.1 MCP server only plans and validates; CLI mutation belongs to future RustUse tooling.
Future CLI flows can use rustuse.toml and rustuse-lock.toml to make copied code maintenance explicit.
